matrix.ac_text: Given I am an Admin, when I access the user registration form, then I should be able to enter user details and select a role.
Given I submit valid user details, when I click "Create User", then the user should be created and assigned the selected role.
Given I assign a role, when the user logs in, then their access should be limited to their assigned role.
Given I try to register a user with an existing email, when I submit the form, then I should see an error message.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to access the user registration form, then I should be denied access.
Given a user is created, when I view the user list, then the new user should appear with the correct role.
Given I am on the login page, when I enter valid credentials, then I should be logged in and redirected to the dashboard.
Given I am logged in, when I click "Logout", then my session should end and I should be redirected to the login page.
Given I enter invalid credentials, when I attempt to log in, then I should see an error message.
Given I am logged out, when I try to access a protected page, then I should be redirected to the login page.
Given I am logged in, when my session expires, then I should be automatically logged out.
Given I am logged in, when I refresh the page, then my session should persist if not expired. ---
Given I am an Admin, when I view the user list, then I should see options to activate or deactivate users.
Given a user is deactivated, when they attempt to log in, then they should be denied access.
Given a user is activated, when they attempt to log in, then they should be granted access.
Given I deactivate a user, when I confirm the action, then the user’s status should update to "Inactive".
Given I activate a user, when I confirm the action, then the user’s status should update to "Active".
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to activate or deactivate a user, then I should be denied access. ---
Given I am on the login page, when I click "Forgot Password", then I should be able to request a password reset.
Given I request a password reset, when I provide my email, then I should receive a reset link if the email is registered.
Given I am logged in, when I access the change password form, then I should be able to set a new password.
Given I set a new password, when I submit, then the password must meet the complexity requirements.
Given I enter a password that does not meet policy, when I submit, then I should see a validation error.
Given I successfully change my password, when I log in again, then I should use the new password. ---
Given I enter invalid credentials, when I attempt to log in, then the failed attempt count should increase.
Given I reach 5 failed attempts, when I try to log in again, then my account should be locked.
Given my account is locked, when I try to log in, then I should see a lockout message.
Given my account is locked, when an Admin views my profile, then they should see the lockout status.
Given my account is locked, when I reset my password, then my account should be unlocked.
Given I have not reached 5 failed attempts, when I enter valid credentials, then I should be able to log in. ---
Given I am logged in, when I access my profile, then I should see my current contact information and preferences.
Given I update my contact info, when I save changes, then my profile should reflect the new information.
Given I update my preferences, when I save changes, then my preferences should be applied.
Given I enter invalid data, when I try to save, then I should see validation errors.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to access the profile page, then I should be redirected to login.
Given I update my profile, when I view my audit log, then the change should be recorded. ---
Given I am logged in, when I am inactive for 15 minutes, then I should be automatically logged out.
Given I am logged out due to inactivity, when I try to perform an action, then I should be redirected to login.
Given I am logged in, when I interact with the system, then the inactivity timer should reset.
Given I am logged out due to inactivity, when I log in again, then I should return to the dashboard.
Given the session timeout is configurable, when an Admin changes the value, then the new timeout should apply to all users.
Given I am logged in, when I receive a timeout warning, then I should be able to extend my session. ---
Given MFA is enabled, when I log in, then I should be prompted for a second authentication factor.
Given I provide the correct second factor, when I submit, then I should be logged in.
Given I provide an incorrect second factor, when I submit, then I should see an error message.
Given MFA is not enabled, when I log in, then I should not be prompted for a second factor.
Given I am an Admin, when I configure MFA settings, then the changes should apply to all users.
Given I lose access to my second factor, when I request help, then I should be able to reset MFA with Admin assistance. ---
Given I am a Teller, when I log in, then I should be prompted to open a teller session.
Given I open a session, when I assign a cash drawer, then the session should start and be recorded.
Given I have an open session, when I try to open another, then I should be prevented.
Given I close my session, when I confirm, then the session should end and be logged.
Given I have an open session, when I log out, then I should be prompted to close the session.
Given I am not a Teller, when I try to open a teller session, then I should be denied access. ---
Given I have an open teller session, when I perform a cash-in, then the transaction should be recorded and my balance updated.
Given I have an open teller session, when I perform a cash-out, then the transaction should be recorded and my balance updated.
Given I perform a balance verification, when I request it, then I should see my current cash drawer balance.
Given I try to transact without an open session, then I should be prevented.
Given I perform a transaction, when I view my transaction log, then the transaction should appear.
Given I exceed my transaction limit, when I attempt a transaction, then I should receive an alert. ---
Given I am a Teller, when I view my profile, then I should see my transaction limits.
Given I attempt a transaction within my limit, when I submit, then the transaction should proceed.
Given I attempt a transaction above my limit, when I submit, then I should receive an alert and the transaction should be blocked.
Given I reach my daily limit, when I attempt another transaction, then I should be prevented.
Given my transaction limit changes, when I view my profile, then the new limit should be displayed.
Given I am a Manager, when a Teller exceeds their limit, then I should receive a notification. ---
Given I am a Teller, when I view my transaction log, then I should see options to reverse or void eligible transactions.
Given I reverse a transaction, when I confirm, then the reversal should be recorded and the balance adjusted.
Given I void a transaction, when I confirm, then the void should be recorded and the balance adjusted.
Given I reverse or void a transaction, when I view the audit log, then the action should be logged.
Given I try to reverse a non-eligible transaction, then I should be prevented.
Given I reverse a transaction, when I view my balance, then it should reflect the change. ---
Given I am a Teller, when I access my activity log, then I should see all my transactions for the day.
Given I perform end-of-day reconciliation, when I confirm, then my session should close and a report should be generated.
Given I have discrepancies, when I reconcile, then I should be prompted to provide a reason.
Given I complete reconciliation, when I view my log, then the reconciliation should be recorded.
Given I am a Manager, when a Teller completes reconciliation, then I should be notified.
Given I am not a Teller, when I try to access teller activity logs, then I should be denied access. ---
Given I have an open session, when I try to open another, then I should be prevented.
Given I close my session, when I try to open a new one, then I should be allowed.
Given I am a Manager, when I view teller sessions, then I should see only one active session per teller.
Given I have an open session, when I log out, then I should be prompted to close the session.
Given I have no open session, when I log in, then I should be prompted to open one.
Given I am not a Teller, when I try to open a teller session, then I should be denied. ---
Given I am logged in, when I access the dashboard, then I should see real-time KPIs.
Given there are active users, when I view the dashboard, then the count should be accurate.
Given there are transactions today, when I view the dashboard, then the numbers should reflect actual data.
Given there are pending approvals, when I view the dashboard, then I should see the count.
Given I refresh the dashboard, when new data is available, then the KPIs should update.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to access the dashboard, then I should be redirected to login. ---
Given I am logged in, when a system alert occurs, then I should receive a notification.
Given there is a teller discrepancy, when I am a Manager, then I should be notified.
Given I have pending actions, when I log in, then I should see notifications.
Given I dismiss a notification, when I refresh, then it should not reappear.
Given I click a notification, when it relates to an action, then I should be taken to the relevant page.
Given I am not logged in, when a notification is generated, then I should not receive it. ---
Given I am logged in, when I access the dashboard, then I should see quick access widgets.
Given I click a widget, when it represents an operation, then I should be taken to the relevant page.
Given I have permissions for an operation, when I use a widget, then the operation should be available.
Given I do not have permissions, when I use a widget, then I should be denied access.
Given I customize my dashboard, when I save, then my widget preferences should persist.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to access widgets, then I should be redirected to login. ---
Given I am an Admin, when I access the account creation form, then I should be able to enter KYC details.
Given I submit valid KYC details, when I create an account, then the account should be created and listed.
Given I enter invalid or incomplete KYC, when I submit, then I should see validation errors.
Given I create an account, when I view the account list, then the new account should appear.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to create an account, then I should be denied access.
Given I create an account, when I view the audit log, then the action should be recorded. ---
Given I am logged in, when I access the account list, then I should see search and filter options.
Given I enter a customer name, when I search, then matching accounts should be displayed.
Given I filter by status, when I select a status, then only accounts with that status should appear.
Given I filter by branch, when I select a branch, then only accounts from that branch should appear.
Given I clear filters, when I view the list, then all accounts should be shown.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to search accounts, then I should be redirected to login. ---
Given I am an Admin, when I view an account, then I should see options to change its status.
Given I change an account status, when I confirm, then the status should update accordingly.
Given I set an account to dormant or closed, when I view the account, then the new status should be displayed.
Given I try to transact on a dormant or closed account, then I should be prevented.
Given I change an account status, when I view the audit log, then the action should be recorded.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to change account status, then I should be denied access. ---
Given I am logged in, when I access the account list, then I should see only accounts permitted by my role.
Given I am an Admin, when I access the account list, then I should see all accounts.
Given I am a Teller, when I access the account list, then I should see only assigned accounts.
Given I try to access a restricted account, then I should be denied.
Given my role changes, when I log in again, then my account access should update.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to access accounts, then I should be redirected to login. ---
Given I am logged in, when I attempt a transaction, then only active accounts should be selectable.
Given I select a dormant or closed account, when I try to transact, then I should be prevented.
Given I transact on an active account, when I submit, then the transaction should proceed.
Given an account status changes to inactive, when I try to transact, then I should be blocked.
Given I view the account list, when I see account statuses, then only active accounts should allow transactions.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to transact, then I should be redirected to login. ---
Given I am an Admin, when I access branch management, then I should see options to add or edit branches.
Given I add a branch, when I submit valid details, then the branch should be created and listed.
Given I edit a branch, when I save changes, then the branch details should update.
Given I enter invalid details, when I submit, then I should see validation errors.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to manage branches, then I should be denied access.
Given I add or edit a branch, when I view the audit log, then the action should be recorded. ---
Given I am an Admin, when I access system configuration, then I should see options for working hours, holidays, and transaction limits.
Given I update a parameter, when I save, then the new value should apply system-wide.
Given I enter invalid values, when I save, then I should see validation errors.
Given I update transaction limits, when I save, then Tellers should see the new limits.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to configure parameters, then I should be denied access.
Given I update a parameter, when I view the audit log, then the change should be recorded. ---
Given I am an Admin, when I access security settings, then I should see options for password policy, session timeout, and allowed IPs.
Given I update the password policy, when I save, then all users should be required to comply.
Given I update session timeout, when I save, then the new timeout should apply to all users.
Given I update allowed IPs, when I save, then only those IPs should be able to access the system.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to change security settings, then I should be denied access.
Given I update a security setting, when I view the audit log, then the change should be recorded. ---
Given I am logged in, when I access language settings, then I should see all supported languages.
Given I select a language, when I save, then the system UI should update to that language.
Given I log in again, when I have a preferred language, then the UI should default to that language.
Given I select an unsupported language, when I save, then I should see an error.
Given I am not logged in, when I try to change language, then I should be redirected to login.
Given I change my language, when I view the UI, then all labels and messages should be translated. ---
Given a critical action occurs, when I view the audit log, then the action should be recorded with timestamp and user.
Given I am an Admin, when I access the audit log, then I should see all critical actions.
Given I am not an Admin, when I try to access the audit log, then I should be denied.
Given I perform a non-critical action, when I view the audit log, then it should not appear.
Given I search the audit log, when I enter criteria, then matching actions should be displayed.
Given I export the audit log, when I confirm, then I should receive a file with the log data. ---
Given I access the system, when I view the URL, then it should use HTTPS.
Given I submit sensitive data, when I inspect the network, then the data should be encrypted.
Given I am an Admin, when I review system configuration, then encryption should be enabled.
Given I try to access the system over HTTP, then I should be redirected to HTTPS.
Given I am a user, when I use the system, then my session cookies should be secure.
Given I am a security auditor, when I test the system, then all endpoints should require encryption. ---
